column1: # Lines starting with '#' are comments. canonical_en is the English string
# that the recognition pipeline already understands; translation is the
# localized OCR text that should map to it. Lookup is case-insensitive.
#   space_slot       — full SPACE EQ row label (Fore Weapons
#   ground_slot      — full GROUND EQ row label (Body Armor
#   eq_word_first    — first-line of a 2-line EQ label (Fore
#   eq_word_second   — second-line of a 2-line EQ label (Weapons
#   screen_header    — full section header for screen-type detection
#                      (canonical_en MUST be one of the lowercase phrases
# To add a language: append rows with the appropriate language code
# To fix a misrecognition: add a new row with the OCR-typical text and the
# German: SPACE EQ slot row labels
# Full labels — feed GERMAN_ORDER (row index) and _SPACE_EQ_LABELS (screen type).
# canonical_en must match STD_ORDER keys for GERMAN_ORDER to work.

column1: # German: EQ classifier tokens (used by the 2-line composite matcher)
# First-line tokens — map to canonical English first-word.
# Second-line tokens — map to canonical English second-word.
# Single-token keywords — full label on one OCR line.
# canonical_en must be one of the lowercase phrases in text_extractor.py
# Used to translate localized ship-type strings to English before ShipDB lookup.
# Each row maps a single German word (as seen by OCR in the type line) to its
# English canonical STO ship classification word. Hyphens in compound names like
# Hull types — core STO ship classification vocabulary
